entries.name: Commit
entries.prompt: Commit all changes to the branch remote. Write a meaningful, concise commit message that summarizes what changed — not just "bump version". If the only change is a version bump with no other commits, "bump" is fine; otherwise summarize the release or changes.

entries.name: Release
entries.prompt: Bump the version, commit all changes to the develop remote, and then merge develop into main to start a new release. Use a meaningful, concise commit message that summarizes the release contents (e.g. the key changes since the last release) — not just "bump version". If it's only a version bump with no other changes, "bump" is fine.

entries.name: Add tests
entries.prompt: Analyze the recent changes and add comprehensive tests. Cover happy paths, edge cases, and error scenarios. Follow existing test patterns in the codebase.

entries.name: Fix lint
entries.prompt: Check for and fix all linting errors in changed files.

entries.name: Refactor
entries.prompt: Review the recent changes and refactor for clarity, simplicity, and maintainability. No behavior changes.

entries.name: Complete & Merge
entries.prompt: Merge the worktree branch at {{path}} into {{baseBranch}}. Commit all pending changes first, then merge and resolve any conflicts. After successful merge, confirm completion. Use meaningful, concise commit/merge messages that summarize what changed — not just "merge branch".

entries.name: Merge worktree
entries.prompt: cd "{{projectPath}}" && git fetch origin && git checkout {{baseBranch}} && git pull --ff-only && git merge --no-ff {{name}} -m "Merge {{name}} into {{baseBranch}}"

entries.name: Cleanup worktree
entries.prompt: cd "{{projectPath}}" && git worktree remove .worktrees/{{name}} && git branch -d {{name}}
